target.name: Arbe Robotics
target.founder: Kobi Marenko, Noam Arkind, Oz Fixman

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Arbe Robotics operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector as a public-stage company that has raised $118M to date. The valuation universe for this company is constrained: while five private peers operate within Drones & Autonomous—including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Elroy Air—no public comparables exist in this category. The private peer set totals six companies, though meaningful same-stage and valuation-tier clustering remains sparse.

The peer-median approach, typically the primary triangulation method, is not applicable here. The narrow peer universe within Drones & Autonomous yields only two same-stage companies and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier peers—falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable median-based analysis. A fallback to broader sector medians would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity across drone, autonomous vehicle, and robotics subsegments. The absence of public comparables further limits benchmarking options.

Without sufficient peer density, a robust triangulated valuation range cannot be supported by the available data. Additional methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis, precedent transaction benchmarking, or venture-backed exit multiples—would be required to establish defensible valuation bounds. Key uncertainties include Arbe's near-term path to profitability, competitive positioning relative to the five named peers, and the sustainability of its capital efficiency at $118M raised.
